published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011: Aumann/random-set sharp regions for convex moment prediction models; https://www.econometricsociety.org/publications/econometrica/2011/11/01/sharp-identification-regions-models-convex-moment-predictions

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: shared-completion strict-containment kernel; pid_mediation_proxy/v1: adjacent nonrectangular direct/indirect geometry

published_prior_art.cite: HuLiWager2022 defines direct/indirect decomposition but not latent-map joint nonrectangularity; https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/109/4/1165/6524620

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: capacitated-incidence certificate characterization for hidden-edge endpoints

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 supplies random-set separating inequalities; classical b-matching/cover certificate literature is not yet cited

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Fixed-map finite LP sharpness is routine once the exposure map is known; it is not a novel theorem beyond AronowSamii2017 plus bounded-outcome partial-ID algebra.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The generic open-class nonrectangularity claim gives only ranges for the K=2,n=4 witness, not a concrete noncorner observed law with specified probabilities and outcome means.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: HuLiWager2022 and SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 are cited as comparators without section/equation/page anchors tying them to the specific joint-nonrectangularity gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The certificate claim invokes b-matching/set-cover structure but omits classical matching and cover-inequality prior art such as Edmonds 1965 and Wolsey 1975.

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: Assessed tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the current kernel needs a sharper published-comparator frontier, not only a network-specialized random-set envelope.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Theta_I(P)=C_K(P) is essentially the closed-convex-hull/support-function representation because C_K is defined from h_K over the same feasible triples.

soundness_flags.label: Locked parameters / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The fixed dimension K+K(K-1) conflicts with feasible maps whose normalized labels have n_k=0 or n_kl=0; the proposal says such components are not reported but still stacks them in theta_DI.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Conjecture 1 with a non-tautological strict-extension statement, e.g. an explicit theorem that the sparse K-support envelope is strictly tighter than a named fixed/cellwise exposure-map relaxation on an open class.
Give one fully numeric K=2,n=4 witness law for Conjecture 2, including assignment probabilities, hidden-edge candidates, label propensities, and outcome cell means.
Totalize or re-index theta_DI so absent labels are handled uniformly across feasible exposure maps.
Add location-level anchors for HuLiWager2022, SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026, and BMM, plus Edmonds/Wolsey-style certificate comparators for Conjecture 3.

recommended_next_step: Revise toward a flagship kernel by fixing the label-coordinate well-posedness issue and replacing the support-function tautology with a strict, anchored comparator-frontier plus a concrete noncorner witness.
